title: Perfect Is a Trap. Real Is the Win.
subtitle: An affirmation for releasing the perfect-home standard
excerpt: An affirmation for when the gap between the home as it is and the home as it should be is making the actual home feel wrong. Use it to name the real standard: a home that is lived in, specific, and yours.

practice.whenToUse: Use when scrolling through beautiful home images has left your own home feeling like a failure.

content.text: The perfect home in any magazine or feed contains no evidence that anyone lives in it. No toast crumbs, no wet towels, no pile of shoes by the door. The "perfect" standard it sets is the standard of an unoccupied house. A home that is actually lived in cannot meet it, and should not try.
Perfect is a trap. Real is the win.

Say it when the comparison thought comes in. Say it in the morning when the house looks like a house someone slept in. Say it when a visitor is coming and the urge to make the house look empty arrives.

The real home is the one with your books stacked on the table and your shoes by the door and the throw your kids have claimed. It is the one people come to and stay in. That is what the affirmation is naming: lived-in as a standard, not as a failure.
